mnemonics: DR. DEMO: Deep Reinforcement Learning Demonstrates Models' Operations
keyPoints: A demo showcases a trained model performing a specific task in a simulated or real-world environment
It is used to validate the model's performance, identify edge cases, and communicate results to stakeholders
The complexity can range from a simple script to a full interactive application with a user interface
commonMistakes: Demoing an overfit model that performs well on training data but fails in a live environment
Failing to properly set up the environment, leading to crashes or incorrect behavior during the demonstration
Not having a clear objective or narrative for the demo, making it difficult for the audience to understand the model's capabilities
